metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: The Lost Caverns of Ixalan emphasizes tempo-driven gameplay. This is a rough format to fall behind in due to efficient creatures and evasive threats. The format values early board control and punishes slow starts.

color_notes.{W} White: Best color. Efficient cheap creatures and strong common removal.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Close second — Waterwind Scout is the format's top common.
color_notes.{R} Red: Strong efficient creatures and flexible removal like Abrade.
color_notes.{G} Green: Good value creatures but slower early game.
color_notes.{B} Black: Weakest color with lackluster creatures despite decent removal.

key_gameplay_notes: Format prioritizes efficient tempo plays and punishes stumbles.
Most decks want 16-17 lands; 17-18 with taplands, higher curve, draw spells, or mana sinks.
Even controlling decks need plenty of cheap 1-3 drops.
Aim for one of the top-four archetypes (Azorius Artifacts, Izzet Pirates, Gruul Dinos, Boros Midrange).

mechanics.name: Descend
mechanics.summary: Threshold mechanic that triggers when 4 or more cards are in your graveyard.
mechanics.performance: Featured prominently in Dimir, Golgari, and Rakdos strategies — strongest in Dimir.

mechanics.name: Explore
mechanics.summary: Reveal the top card; if it's a land, put it in hand, otherwise put a +1/+1 counter on the creature and choose whether to keep the card on top or in the graveyard.
mechanics.performance: Card-draw engine used in Simic decks.

mechanics.name: Craft
mechanics.summary: Artifact-assembly mechanic that rewards artifact synergy. Anchors Azorius Artifacts.

mechanics.name: Discover
mechanics.summary: Fixed cascade mechanic — cast a 2-3 mana spell for free. Featured on Etali's Favor and others.

mechanics.name: Cycling
mechanics.summary: Utility mechanic enabling card cycling and self-mill strategies.

mechanics.name: Sacrifice
mechanics.summary: Central to Orzhov's incremental value plan.

mechanics.name: Caves
mechanics.summary: Subtheme — dedicating to caves requires 7+ to enable payoffs. Sinuous Benthisaur is splashable with just 3 caves.

tier_1.name: Azorius Artifacts / Fliers
tier_1.strategy: Cheap flying creatures paired with artifact synergies.

tier_1.name: Izzet Pirates
tier_1.strategy: Aggressive tempo deck with cheap creatures, evasion, and haste.

tier_1.name: Gruul Dinosaurs
tier_1.strategy: Ramp and trade early, then deploy large threats.

tier_2.name: Simic Explore
tier_2.strategy: Midrange with card advantage through the explore mechanic.

tier_2.name: Dimir Descend
tier_2.strategy: Self-mill strategy around Descend. Best payoffs are rares (Wail of the Forgotten, The Ancient One, The Everflowing Well).

tier_2.name: Orzhov Sacrifice
tier_2.strategy: Build incremental advantage through sacrifice outlets.

key_cards.name: Tarrian's Journal
key_cards.type_line: Legendary Artifact — Book // Legendary Land — Cave

tier_3.name: Rakdos Descend
tier_3.strategy: Triggers from descending actions, but lacks quality payoffs.

caves_archetype.description: Dedicate to caves with 7+ to enable major payoffs. Sinuous Benthisaur is splashable at just 3 caves.

sideboard_tips: Include graveyard hate (Pit of Offerings) versus Descend decks.
Pack artifact/enchantment removal (Deconstruction Hammer) for craft-heavy decks.
Add extra removal for bomb-heavy matchups.
general_tips: Prioritize cards that are good everywhere — efficiency beats archetype-only payoffs.
Aim to land in one of the top-four archetypes when possible.
Don't fall behind — the format punishes slow starts brutally.
Even controlling decks need cheap 1-3 drops to survive early.
